page: Hetzner Cloud cpx51 is an x86_64 virtual server powered by the QEMU hypervisor. It features 16 shared AMD EPYC virtual CPUs operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 31.0 GB of system memory, providing approximately 1.95 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled with 360 GB of SSD capacity. No dedicated GPU resources are included.

Benchmark evaluations reveal a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance is categorized in the bottom 10%, reflecting the shared nature of the CPU allocation. However, multi-core performance metrics, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores, fall within the average range. Memory bandwidth tests for both cached and uncached read/write operations also demonstrate average performance. Text processing is a relative bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25%, while tasks like software compilation, ray tracing, and image processing achieve average tier results.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the cpx51 offers a high density of virtual cores and memory within a shared-resource framework, making it a viable option for workloads that can tolerate variable CPU performance. It is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, static web serving, Redis caching, and moderate database operations. However, it is less appropriate for single-threaded tasks or latency-sensitive applications due to its poor single-core performance.
description: Hetzner Cloud cpx51 is an x86_64 virtual server built on the QEMU hypervisor, featuring 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, 31.0 GB of RAM, and 360 GB of local SSD storage. Because the CPU allocation is shared, single-core performance is limited, scoring in the bottom 10% on stress-ng benchmarks. However, the server delivers average multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operation speeds. This resource profile offers a cost-effective option for multi-threaded workloads that do not require dedicated CPU cores. It is suitable for static web serving, software compilation, Redis caching, and general-purpose multi-threaded applications, though it is not recommended for single-threaded or latency-critical tasks.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud cpx51 is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 31.0 GB of RAM, and 360 GB of local SSD storage, delivering balanced multi-core performance for budget-conscious workloads.

tagline: A budget-friendly shared virtual server offering sixteen AMD EPYC cores and balanced multi-core performance for multi-threaded workloads.

31.0 GB of system memory and 360 GB local SSD storage
Average multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmark results
Poor single-core performance due to shared CPU allocation
Suitable for static web serving, software compilation, and Redis caching
